reviews.review_description:
Actually, that's kind of misleading. It is a typical adventure game from the 80's and 90's.... and that's a good thing. The games in those days were products of love, innovation and involved good story telling and tough (albeit sometimes illogical) puzzles. The cave graphics and atmosphere are excellent and immersive.  Game play is straight forward, but try the different movement methods to see which one works best for you. I tried Roberta's favorite method and, sorry Roberta, but it's awful. Classic works best for me. When the game was first offered at $40 I steered clear, but now that there have been extensive updates and it's on sale, I figured I'd give it a try and I'm glad I did.

Roberta, don't give up on the VR platform. Your first foray had a rocky start, but you got it right after some updates.  I hope there are more, even better projects in the works.

reviews.review_title: Adaptation of a classic by a legend
reviews.review_description: I was really delighted to see this. As a kid, the text-only version of this game (called Microsoft Adventure) was the first and only game on the PC, much to our chagrin (the PC was at the time being marketed as a business machine). So we spent hours of our childhood playing this, and of course building a mental picture of the cave in our minds. Then as a young co-op, my coworkers and I discovered this game on the mainframe computer at work. Lol. That was a blast. To give you an idea of how classic this game is, there is someone in my neighborhood who literally has the license plate "XYZZY" (a central feature of the game). Lol. And Roberta Williams, the creator of the "King's Quest" series is such a hero. When I was a kid, one day I was playing King's Quest, my father walked in, glanced at the screen, and just said dryly: "This game was written by a *woman* wasn't it?" Yes. Yes, it was. And it was so awesome. So I was delighted to see this game. I haven't played it through, yet, but I am really enjoying it and it's really interesting to see how Williams and company imagined the cave. I can't speak to how someone who had never played the original would find this game, but someone who has a history with the original would I think really enjoy it.

This game is exactly what I was looking for. Patient, relaxing exploration, with some excitement and puzzles, but no running for your life or breaking your brain (although one of the mazes was quite a challenge!). The cave system is hugely extensive, with lots of amazing, well-designed rooms to discover. The Soft Room was an especially pleasant surprise.

I wanted to update my score to reflect the patches that were done, and the lower cost compared to when it first launched.

----

This feels old, and not in a good retro way. I nearly fell for the retro vibes, but there are too many annoying things that got in the way.

This feels like it was designed by someone who has never played a VR game before. Movement is locked to the direction you start walking in, but if you turn in real life, you still continue on in your original direction unless you stop and then start walking again.

While smooth rotation via the analog stick is available, it's very slow, and you can't change the rotation speed. This is really bad from a comfort point of view. Slow rotation is an easy way to induce motion sickness - faster speeds are generally more comfortable for a lot of people.

The entire interface has been lifted from a flat screen and transplanted into VR. You literally move an icon over what you want to look at/interact with, and can't physically pick things up in your hand to examine them.

That’s what I thought! Started with the Zorkian intro. Nice difficult to watch video. Then here we go to the dungeon. The graphics are limited and moving around sucks.  Abandon all hope you who enter the CC! Don’t waste your time or your money!

What a fantastic way to explore cave systems. Great graphics, easy control system. I thought I'd just give it 30 mins but I got so into it that after nearly 2 hours my battery died.

Give it a go A+

reviews.review_title: Outside of 1 or 2 cons, I love it!
reviews.review_description:
I'm not one that's ever had any interest in text adventure games, but I've definitely been enjoying this VR rendition of the original. The first of it's kind in bith forms.
The graphics are great and di the job, and the audio even better. The puzzles and general ganeplay are also good thus far from what I've played, though occasionally dome items can be a nuisance with trying to figure out ehat to do with them as there is basically zero indication of use (example being the star rod to make a bridge... there is literally nothing ti hint at that and even less reason anyone would think to even try that. Making many parts just random illogical guess work). 
The biggest issue of the gane o far is the odd idea to make it a 100% point-and-click when bringing this text based dventure (not a point & click one) into 3D, especially VR. So there is absolutely zero physical interactions beyond you pointing a laser pointer from your and and... clicking. Even o open doors and climb ladders. Very unnecessary and hurts an therwise seemingly great journey through this well crafted cave.
I would also say that I hate the randomness of the game like the dwarf encounters. Death should never be s random event in a game you have zero control over. Somrle things that worked in text adventures do not translate to modern 3D gaming, but luckily you csn ssve at ANY time, so I just make a habit of saving every other minute.
As for controls, I think thrley are perfect after seitching to regular 1st person control and head-based movement. I did notice that it seems to switch back to controller movement direction upon first starting my game again though, so hopfully that doesnt keep happening.

Beyond all f that though, it seems almost 5-Star worthy so far with only the point and click interaction system and occasional semi-illogical puzzle/ randomness holding it back fir me. 
I'm very happy to have finally tried this game and do enjoy my time with it.

Got this on meta 3 , read a few reviews saying control system not good , I have no problems . I have in the past played text-based adventures , but not this one . To see a VR conversation of a classic is just fabulous . It's full of nostalgia for me .picked it up for 11 quid, so it represents great value for money.  The puzzles are deliberately not easy  ( but plenty of help  on the internet if you're really stuck ) . The automap feature is a gem . This is a easy going adventure, not an adrenaline based shooter . Ideal for fans of the old text based products .

developer_response.body:
Yep! We launched the game with less control options.  VR is fairly new, and definitely new to us.  Over the past year, we've added a WIDE variety of control options, detailed tweaking, speed controls, look controls, vignetting, and with the Quest 3, we've added a mixed reality option.  It's a significantly improved game, almost to the point of being a new game, if you include the upgrades to the art, coloring, lighting, animations, easter eggs, sounds, etc.  

We're thrilled you're enjoying it and that it's giving you a blast of nostalgia.  It certainly is a game for the ages!

Thank you for the kind words!

reviews.review_description: I loved the original txt versions of all these adventures way back in the 80’s on my BBC micro and later on PC.  Are you converting any of the other txt adventures ? Would be fantastic if all were done.

reviews.review_title: I am Roberta Williams - About rel 2.0
reviews.review_description: When evaluating this game, please consider the following. Our initial release received some negative feedback. I will confess that it was well deserved. It was our first VR release and we had some learning to do. Over the last 11 months we have continued working on our VR release and put a huge amount of effort into addressing every concern we saw in any review. The list of updates is huge: We added additional modes of locomotion, integrated tutorials, autosave, new graphics, more sound effects, footstep sounds, performance enhancements, etc. The game that is available today is not the same as what we initially released. Thank you to everyone who has played the game or will play the game. I'm very proud of the game and hope you will enjoy the game!

Unintuitive controls, Terrible navigation. 
Fascinating cave but Solutions to puzzles are illogical. 

Random dwarf randomly killed me with no chance to defend myself. Then was back at the house. Nope. I’m out. On to the next game. Save your money.

developer_response.body:
We're sorry for your experience, but the controls can be set and calibrated to your individual tastes.  There's a compass and auto-mapping system.  The puzzles in the game were written in the mid 1970's for 2 young girls.  At the time, they were the first puzzles in an adventure game, ever.  By and large, they still hold up to this day, but there's no questioning that some portion of this experience is simply to dive back to the origin of adventure games.  The game is a blast to play.  The history and some of its retro-sharp edges are very much a part of its charm!  

Apologies, if this wasn't your cup of tea, but we're continually improving the comfort factors of the game. Look a bit deeper into the history of the game to gain greater insight into our goals, grab your lamp, and give it another shot!

reviews.review_description:
I get it.  Most of the popular games in VR involve guns and violence and murdering anything that moves. So I understand when a game comes around that doesn’t allow you to do that, that people respond with poor reviews.

In full transparency, I’m 50 years old and grew up playing text adventures, dreaming of a day when something like VR would be a reality. 

To Meta, and anyone else who cares, THIS type of game is the reason I bought a headset to begin with. It’s also the reason why when I want to play an immersive RPG that I continue to fire up Skyrim (and I know you need a PC to play it). Anyway, this game is one of the few rare games on the Quest that captures that feeling for me.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing our game! It's good to see people that have played games since the text era in which ADVENT originally came out in still playing in Virtual Reality headsets. We're really glad you enjoyed the game in the spirit which we created it! I'll be sure to pass along your words to the rest of the team.

reviews.review_description: If you have ever enjoyed playing old text based adventure games, this game is an absolute treat.  As an old guy, it is amazing to walk through a world I was introduced to as a young child.  It is a beautiful world to explore.  It feels like the exact same game, just now, I am in the game.  Definitely some ready player one (the book) vibes going on.  It may not be for everyone.  I know my kids most likely won't appreciate what was intended in the making of the game.  But it is the original game.  Nothing changed, just...alive.  It isn't like anything I have played before and I love that about it.

reviews.review_title: It's a hard sell for lots of reasons.
reviews.review_description:
I like (or maybe want to like) Colossal Cave,but there are multiple barriers to entry that I think may scare most people away and I can totally understand where those people are coming from.  It doesn't feel like a modern-day puzzler, sharing much more in common with those late 90's First-person endeavours and that in combination with the dated visuals and lack of VR interaction will chase away casual players. Add to that some very convoluted puzzles and trial and error solutions that can feel unfair at times and this can be a hard sell.  There’s also the rather lofty $40 price tag which I audibly scoffed at when I first saw it as I expected somewhere around half of that after playing a few failed runs.  There's a lengthy puzzler here with plenty of replay value but for all the issues I mentioned above, and that asking price, I find this hard to recommend this to anyone but the most passionate of puzzle solvers or those who want to experience a piece of gaming history through a modern lens...or 2 lenses I guess.

VIDEO REVIEW - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uMt7iE07pDs

WRITTEN REVIEW - https://www.thevrgrid.com/colossal-cave/

developer_response.body: Greetings! I'd encourage you to talk another look at the game, and even let me know if you'd like a beta of what we have coming. We've taken everyone's comments seriously and been continuously enhancing the game. Our recent reviews show that we're nailing it, and there is a lot more coming! My email address is: ken at cygnusentertainment.com. I hope you'll take a new look. As you'll see, we've been busy!

reviews.review_description:
You find yourself in an odd situation, playing a game with inexcusably bad mechanics. You wonder why a VR remake released in 2023 is emulating immersion-shattering game controls from point-and-click DOS-based adventures when the original game, released in 1976 was a text-based game with no pointing or clicking.

You wonder why the developers have chosen to add unnecessary friction to the whole experience at every step, and why you lost your first one-hour session because the game doesn't even have auto-save. 

Okay, I'll stop with the game voice. 

The face is that this is a terrible port that purposely avoids making use of the VR medium to which it's been ported, and you're being asked to pay $39.99 for what amounts to clunky nostalgia bait. The whole game is about exploring, finding objects and treasures, running into obstacles, and using the objects you found to get past the obstacles. None of this is done in a particularly enjoyable fashion. 

For a video review, search YouTube for Colossal Cave 6DOF Reviews.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback. We have studied every review that was short of 5 stars, including your own, and been continually enhancing the product. The tide has turned and our most recent reviews are in the 5 star category. I'd encourage you to consider revisiting the product. We also have a new release coming in the next few weeks that adds teleport and a bunch more. Email me (ken at cygnusentertainment.com) if you'd want to beta test the latest. Best wishes, Ken Williams

reviews.review_description:
The game takes you back in time to the original text adventure.
The graphics give shape to what once could only be imagined.
It is clear that this title will appeal more to elderly gamers like me. The new recruits could snub it and consider it anachronistic.
A negative note on the movement.
The Quest allows you to physically spin in place without using the stick (this action reduces moreover the motion sickess).
When you do this, you will lose any subtitles stuck in their initial position.
Instead, they should follow the player's movement (as is the case in all vr games).
To fix.
Price a bit high for what it offers.

developer_response.body: I'm so glad you enjoyed the game! You'll find that we've made some updates to locomotion and movement, and more are planned.  Thank you again, for playing the game and taking the time to leave a good, honest review.

reviews.review_title: Just relax and enter the cave
reviews.review_description: Excellent reimagining of a foundational game. It's an adventure and it's colossal. Things in this cave are not what you expect.

The interface isn't easy, perhaps due to trying to keep it old school, but an option to do it in a new modern way might help.
At the end of the game, twice now, I blew up the wall but a bug didn't show it coming down or the office beyond.  I heard her talking and I could walk through an apparently solid wall to see the final, but there is a flaw in the Quest 2 version, anyway.

developer_response.body: Hello!  Thank you for the review and feedback!  As to the controls and interface, there are 3 completely different forms of locomotion that you can select.  There is also a vignetting option, as well as speed controls, including speed for looking and "snapping".  Everything is intended to give you ultimate control over how you engage with the game.  As to the bug ... sadly, we need to own that one.  Whoops!  It snuck into the previous update and existed for just a short time.  We received feedback from players and immediately fixed it.  Anything from version 1.007 and above has the fix.  Thank you for letting us know!  Finally, nice job on making it to the end, twice.  Congratulations!

reviews.review_title: I love the meandering down memory lane
reviews.review_description: I was excited to have one of my first games ever played reimagined for VR. I bought the game as soon as I saw it was available. The story is compelling and the puzzles are challenging. It’s a game you can play over and over again. I prefer the teleport style movement in this game to the smooth glide and I am glad they made that update!

If you like games that don't hold your hand and you love to explore then get this game!

Get a guide (as you'll likely need one). This takes you back to a time when people paid for guides to games (or before then?) But remimagined in a beautiful VR world.

Don't sleep on this gem!

b i t , l y / C C a v e
